name: Ottawa REDBLACKS
traj_note: Trending healthier — Henderson (field HB) back and starting; Parks back in the DL rotation. Lewis week-to-week is the swing.
headline: 0–3, but the availability board reads modest: an elite ratio cushion is intact and the losses sit at import/depth spots with capable replacements. The only real rating drag is Lewis (WR, week-to-week). The record is a QB-form and turnover story — priced separately from this board.

name: Toronto Argonauts
traj_note: Roster mostly stable; the season-ending FS loss is the standing hit and the ratio sits right at the 7-National floor.
headline: Near the bottom of the East under rookie HC Mike Miller. Close to full health at the starter level, but the season-ending loss of FS Priester (covered by a CFL-debut call-up) is the one real hit, and the National ratio sits right at the 7-starter floor.

name: Montréal Alouettes
traj_note: Getting healthier on defence — Beverette (LB) back, Scott III (RB) returns; secondary still short both starting corners.
headline: Atop the East on the strength of Alexander (league passing leader, zero picks) and an elite Philpot. The defence is the soft spot — 89 points allowed through three games and a secondary missing both starting corners.

name: Hamilton Tiger-Cats
traj_note: Deep and calm; two soft spots on defence, offence and ST unchanged across the opening weeks.
headline: Stable and deep, with arguably the league's best QB room (Mitchell + Ford) and a ~10-National starter cushion. Two soft spots on defence: the MIKE linebacker has yet to debut (knee), and an all-star boundary corner missed Week 3 (head), covered by a practice-roster call-up.

name: Saskatchewan Roughriders
traj_note: Championship core intact; the compounding secondary is the season-long vulnerability thread.
headline: The defending Grey Cup champions are built on 40-year-old Trevor Harris, an elite AJ Ouellette ground game, and All-Star corner Tevaughn Campbell. The one crack is a compounding secondary — halfback and safety have churned through injuries (Milligan Jr., Lokombo, Hughes) across the opening weeks.

name: Edmonton Elks
traj_note: Traditionally slow starters; the two rating-relevant dings are both interior 6-game IL losses.
headline: Perennial slow starters with an improved roster around a rejuvenated Cody Fajardo, the complete Justin Rankin at running back, and elite returner Javon Leake. The dings are interior: National RG Woodmansey (knee) and MLB Anderson (hamstring) are both on the 6-game IL.

name: Calgary Stampeders
traj_note: Got their first win at BC in Week 4; the soft spots are the kicking game and a Week-4 secondary hit.
headline: Calgary rides the streaky brilliance of Vernon Adams Jr. and a real inside-outside pass rush (Hutchings + Orimolade). The soft spots are the kicking game — 15-year ironman Paredes on the 6-game IL, rookie McAtamney in — and a secondary thinned by National CB Greene's Week 4 injury.

name: Winnipeg Blue Bombers
traj_note: The calmest roster in the league — offence and secondary unchanged all season; all churn is deep front-seven rotation.
headline: The calmest roster in the league — offence and secondary unchanged all season, an elite National core (Oliveira, Holm, Jefferson), and a ~12-National starter cushion that makes ratio a non-issue. The one genuine weakness isn't an injury: the boundary edge opposite Willie Jefferson is unsettled and replacement-level.
